messages.author: Clinton
messages.text: I just installed the latest APK. I'm in a location that works; when I send a public message using the standard app, I hear two repeats, and I can administer a repeater. When I do a manual ping in this app, I get a "No response - dead zone" message. I cleared the cache/app data. Not sure what could be wrong. I suppose I could try installing the next-to-latest version.
messages.content_html: I just installed the latest APK. I'm in a location that works; when I send a public message using the standard app, I hear two repeats, and I can administer a repeater. When I do a manual ping in this app, I get a "No response - dead zone" message. I cleared the cache/app data. Not sure what could be wrong. I suppose I could try installing the next-to-latest version.

messages.author: Clinton
messages.text: Interesting. Installing the most recent version also said dead spots. I went back to the first version I tried (version 1.0.16) and it is working.
messages.content_html: Interesting. Installing the most recent version also said dead spots. I went back to the first version I tried (version 1.0.16) and it is working.

messages.author: Chuck(WSKZ567)
messages.text: 1.0.26 of my wardrive app has been released. I added a distance traveled feature, screenshot feature, and a colorblind mode, for people who are colorblind. I also adjusted the ping timeout to 30s for discovery mode, so it won't timeout repeaters. https://github.com/mintylinux/Meshcore-Wardrive-Android/releases
messages.content_html: 1.0.26 of my wardrive app has been released. I added a distance traveled feature, screenshot feature, and a colorblind mode, for people who are colorblind. I also adjusted the ping timeout to 30s for discovery mode, so it won't timeout repeaters. https://github.com/mintylinux/Meshcore-Wardrive-Android/releases

A Flutter-based mobile wardriving application for mapping MeshCore mesh network coverage in real-time. - mintylinux/Meshcore-Wardrive-Android

I also thought it would be interesting if the app made noise. I can’t look at the map while I’m driving but some sort of indicator of the ping quality would be nice. Maybe it could be sort of like sonar. Send a chirp on each ping and use another noise based on the reply, if any, with different tones or volumes to suggest signal quality.

messages.author: Clinton
messages.text: How do I do a manual ping? I know it was obvious in an older version, but I don't see an option now.
messages.content_html: How do I do a manual ping? I know it was obvious in an older version, but I don't see an option now.

messages.author: Chuck(WSKZ567)
messages.text: it's the arrow on the top bar on the map screen
messages.content_html: it's the arrow on the top bar on the map screen

messages.author: Clinton
messages.text: What is a sample? Is it the number of times the GPS location was requested, or the number of times I pinged the mesh?
messages.content_html: What is a sample? Is it the number of times the GPS location was requested, or the number of times I pinged the mesh?

messages.author: Chuck(WSKZ567)
messages.text: a sample is a ping. if you enable samples. you will see green or red dots on the map. tap on one, and it will tell you the time that sample was taken, and the signal strength
messages.content_html: a sample is a ping. if you enable samples. you will see green or red dots on the map. tap on one, and it will tell you the time that sample was taken, and the signal strength

messages.author: Hugo
messages.text: Is there a tl;dr of the difference between this Meshcore Wardrive app and eg Meshmapper? At first glance it looks like focusing on "offline first" as well as being very configurable, whereas Meshmapper seems more focused on contributing to a big, common online map with regional controls?
messages.content_html: Is there a tl;dr of the difference between this Meshcore Wardrive app and eg Meshmapper? At first glance it looks like focusing on "offline first" as well as being very configurable, whereas Meshmapper seems more focused on contributing to a big, common online map with regional controls?

messages.author: Clinton
messages.text: I'm not the dev, but I think you've got the basic idea. Meshmapper requires one or more listener nodes to be set up, which makes it a real pain to use when you've got scattered infrastructure that isn't connecting yet. MeshCore Wardrive App also allows you to upload to a common map, save your results, or upload to your own mapping server. The live community map is at https://meshwar-map.pages.dev (edited)
messages.content_html: I'm not the dev, but I think you've got the basic idea. Meshmapper requires one or more listener nodes to be set up, which makes it a real pain to use when you've got scattered infrastructure that isn't connecting yet. MeshCore Wardrive App also allows you to upload to a common map, save your results, or upload to your own mapping server. The live community map is at https://meshwar-map.pages.dev (edited)

messages.author: Clinton
messages.text: Hi @Chuck(WSKZ567) . I did a lot of wardriving today, and uploaded data to the public map. I'm surprised that areas that show up in green on my phone show up in orange on the map. I've looked at a few. One, for example, I see four samples, three of which were success, on the app, but the same square on the map shows 1 sample received and 9 lost for a 10% success rate. In the off chance that the issue was old data for that location, well, one, there should be at least three successes (the map on the phone and online are at the same granularity), and I checked some other squares that were never previously wardriven and also see strange results. Any thoughts? Anything I can provide to help troubleshoot?
messages.content_html: Hi @Chuck(WSKZ567). I did a lot of wardriving today, and uploaded data to the public map. I'm surprised that areas that show up in green on my phone show up in orange on the map. I've looked at a few. One, for example, I see four samples, three of which were success, on the app, but the same square on the map shows 1 sample received and 9 lost for a 10% success rate. In the off chance that the issue was old data for that location, well, one, there should be at least three successes (the map on the phone and online are at the same granularity), and I checked some other squares that were never previously wardriven and also see strange results. Any thoughts? Anything I can provide to help troubleshoot?

messages.author: retromesh
messages.text: Out of interest, does this depend on repeaters advertising?
messages.content_html: Out of interest, does this depend on repeaters advertising?

messages.author: Chuck(WSKZ567)
messages.text: It sends a discovery packet from your companion or mobile repeater to find nearby repeaters. It doesn't use a channel message and repeats to verify a signal
messages.content_html: It sends a discovery packet from your companion or mobile repeater to find nearby repeaters. It doesn't use a channel message and repeats to verify a signal
